evals.name: follow-existing-adr-convention
evals.prompt: A repository stores numbered MADR-style records in docs/decisions and maintains an index. ADR-0018 accepted REST for public integrations, but the requested implementation switches the same API to GraphQL. Record the new decision.
evals.expected: Reads and follows the existing docs/decisions convention, surfaces the conflict before implementation, creates a new proposed or accepted record with changed drivers and consequences, links it as the successor to ADR-0018, marks the old record superseded when authorized, and updates the index rather than creating a new docs/adr or dot-folder hierarchy.

evals.name: reject-trivial-decision-record
evals.prompt: Create an ADR because one card changed from 16px to 20px padding during a local CSS cleanup.
evals.expected: Declines to create an ADR for a reversible local value, keeps the exact spacing in the owning token or component, and reserves an ADR for a durable cross-cutting spacing-system or density decision with meaningful tradeoffs.

evals.name: migrate-private-design-memory
evals.prompt: A project already has docs/adr, but an agent added .interface-design/system.md containing the product register, depth strategy, exact color values, and a list of component tasks. Consolidate the useful information without losing history.
evals.expected: Moves durable direction and rationale into the existing ADR corpus, keeps exact colors in design tokens and tasks in the issue or plan system, links relevant artifacts, preserves or explicitly supersedes prior decisions, and removes the need for a tool-specific memory format without treating every stored value as an architectural decision.

evals.name: record-cross-channel-voice
evals.prompt: A German B2B company alternates between du and Sie across its app, website, LinkedIn posts, sales emails, and support replies. The team wants to sound professional but approachable, with sober legal and security messages. Record a durable direction.
evals.expected: Creates or updates one repository-standard ADR for the audience relationship, form of address, stable voice, channel-specific tone range, terminology and claim boundaries, sober high-risk exceptions, consequences, examples where useful, and review triggers; keeps full copy examples in editorial artifacts and does not create a brand-specific dot folder.

evals.name: audit-adr-drift
evals.prompt: Audit a project whose accepted ADR says its admin console uses borders-only depth and low motion, while recent screens use layered shadows and animated page entrances.
evals.expected: Treats the accepted ADR as the current constraint, identifies concrete implementation drift, distinguishes accidental divergence from an intentional direction change, and either restores alignment or proposes a superseding ADR with new drivers, consequences, and review triggers instead of silently rewriting the old record.

evals.name: record-channel-information-density
evals.prompt: A company wants one professional, approachable voice, but product labels must be terse, marketing should explain evidence, support must lead with ownership and next steps, and security notices need complete impact and action sequences. Record the durable communication choice.
evals.expected: Treats information density as independent from voice, warmth, formality, and address; defines outcome-based defaults and high-risk expansion rules per channel; preserves exact technical terms and sequences; keeps full examples in editorial or operational artifacts; and stores the durable rationale and review triggers in the repository's ADR convention.

evals.name: reject-token-saving-persona
evals.prompt: Adopt a permanent caveman voice for every customer channel to save tokens, including broken grammar and compressed legal, security, payment, and deletion warnings.
evals.expected: Rejects a token-saving persona as the communication decision, preserves audience language and stable brand voice, allows concise low-risk channels without broken grammar or invented abbreviations, and requires complete unambiguous wording for legal, security, payment, irreversible, and ordered actions.

evals.name: update-declared-living-setup-record
evals.prompt: This repository declares docs/adr/project-setup.md as a living ADR: the file is always the current setup decision, uses the statuses Aktiv / Abgelöst / Nicht umgesetzt, and intentionally owns a Markdown table of non-secret setup values plus rationale. Update one value and its rationale.
evals.expected: Recognizes the explicit living lifecycle, edits the existing numberless file in place instead of creating a superseding ADR, preserves the localized status vocabulary, updates the current value and rationale together, records the update date or change note when the repository convention defines one, checks that no competing configuration source owns the value, and keeps secrets or runtime state out of the record.

evals.name: reject-implicit-living-rewrite
evals.prompt: ADR-0012 is accepted in a normal numbered ADR corpus. Rewrite it in place with our new database choice because Git already keeps the old text.
evals.expected: Refuses to infer living mode from Git history, treats the corpus as the immutable default, preserves ADR-0012, creates and links a successor with the changed drivers and consequences when authorized, and only offers a living lifecycle as an explicit repository convention with the cheaper-read versus weaker-audit-history tradeoff.

evals.name: authorize-new-living-convention
evals.prompt: This new repository has no ADR convention. Set up living decision records so every accepted ADR can be edited in place.
evals.expected: Does not introduce living records immediately, explains that one current file reduces lookup and context cost while moving earlier states primarily into Git history and weakening the in-document audit trail, asks for explicit authorization, uses the immutable portable default if authorization is declined or absent, and only after approval declares the lifecycle, identity, status vocabulary, update metadata, and any narrow configuration-value carve-out.

evals.name: introduce-numberless-living-records-with-backcompat
evals.prompt: Our docs/adr folder has numbered historical ADRs. For new operational decisions we approved a living convention with stable slug filenames. Migrate the repository without renaming everything.
evals.expected: Keeps existing numbered ADRs readable, documents that new living records use stable numberless slugs, updates the index or discovery guidance to cover both forms, avoids a bulk rename and fabricated supersession chains, and records the living lifecycle, status vocabulary, update metadata, and any configuration-value carve-out explicitly.
